source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
An asymmetrical periodic pulse train \( v_{in} \) of 10 V amplitude with on-time \( T_{ON} = 1 \,ms \) and off-time \( T_{OFF} = 1 \,\mu s \) is applied to the circuit shown in <image>. The diode \( D_1 \) is ideal.
The difference between the maximum voltage and minimum voltage of the output waveform \( v_o \) (in integer) is __________ V.
extraction_notes: Removed the leading answer-format hint and kept the question body only.
The image field is a PIL image object; no image path/URL/file name is present.

image_dependency: Question explicitly says 'the circuit shown in the figure', so circuit topology is not recoverable from text alone.
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the visual asset is central to solving.
The task depends on identifying circuit elements/connections and diode orientation from the figure.
multi_step: Solving requires combining the pulse-train timing data (T_ON and T_OFF) with the circuit behavior from the figure.
The prompt asks for the difference between maximum and minimum output voltage, implying at least finding two waveform extrema before computing their difference.
Ideal diode behavior must be incorporated along with the circuit response, which suggests multi-condition reasoning rather than direct lookup.
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and the prompt further specifies the result should be an integer voltage.
The requested quantity is well-defined: maximum output minus minimum output.
asset_registry_record shows answer_present=true and registry_passed=true, so the sample is structurally complete for checking.

decision_reasons: Core assets are complete: question, required image, and numeric answer are all present, and registry_passed=true with no recorded asset issues.
The sample has strong evaluation value, with high image dependency (0.97), good multi-step reasoning demand (0.74), and high verifiability (0.91).
Although the circuit topology depends on the figure, that risk is mitigated because the image asset exists and appears usable.
The answer is numeric and well-defined, so despite answer_only_no_derivation, the item remains checkable and suitable for the candidate pool.
